mnemonics.title: DROPOUT Magic
mnemonics.content: Drops Random Outputs Preventing Overfitting Uniformly Throughout - During training, randomly 'drops' (sets to zero) neurons with probability p, forcing the network to learn redundant representations and not rely on any single neuron.

mnemonics.title: BATCH NORM Blueprint
mnemonics.content: Batches Are Trained Correctly, Normalizing Inputs Over Variables - Normalizes layer inputs by subtracting batch mean and dividing by batch standard deviation, with learnable gamma (scale) and beta (shift) parameters.

mnemonics.title: Inverted Dropout
mnemonics.content: Inverted Dropout: In Training, divide by keep probability (1-p) so no division needed at test time - This is the modern implementation where scaling is done during training, making test-time inference simpler.

acronyms.usage: Use when referring to the technique that normalizes inputs using batch statistics. Apply before activation function for best results.
Used during inference for Batch Norm - maintain running averages of mean and variance instead of using batch statistics.
Set between 0.1-0.5 typically. Higher p = more regularization. Common values: 0.2 for input layers, 0.5 for hidden layers.
Learnable parameter that allows Batch Norm to learn identity mapping. If γ = σ (std), output = (x-μ)/σ * σ + β = x + (β-μ), enabling recovery of original distribution.
Learnable parameter that allows Batch Norm to learn identity mapping by shifting the normalized output.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a dense city network where random roads close during training. The city (network) must find alternative routes, making the entire system more robust. At test time, all roads open but with reduced capacity (scaled weights).

Picture students (inputs) in different exams (batches) being normalized to a common scoring scale. Each subject (layer) has its own mean (μ) and standard deviation (σ), with teachers able to adjust (γ, β) the passing marks.

Imagine feeding unexpected cuisine to a chef (layer) trained on Indian food - they'll struggle. Batch Norm ensures each 'chef' gets consistently prepared ingredients regardless of previous layers' 'cooking'.
Visualize many sub-networks (thinned networks) being trained simultaneously. At test time, averaging predictions from all these different 'expert committees' gives robust results.

analogies.analogy: Like a student studying for exams by not relying on a single textbook or teacher. If one source is unavailable (dropped), they can still pass using knowledge from other sources. It's like vaccination - exposing the system to controlled 'weak' versions to build immunity against overfitting.

Like a factory quality control station. Raw products (inputs) come in varying conditions, but before assembly, they're standardized - too small stretched, too large compressed, centered at optimal point. This ensures consistent downstream processing.

Dropout is like diversity training (different perspectives prevent groupthink), while Batch Norm is like coffee before a meeting (calms nerves, stabilizes performance). Together they create a well-regulated, robust team.

Like a professor's running average of student performance over semesters. At final evaluation (inference), they don't judge based on one batch (class) but on their overall experience (running statistics).
